entry.title: Re: Workflow Email Notification not triggered for conditi...
entry.content:
@JuanCarlosDelga Standard workflow. We used standard email templates.

WCB_CC_RELEASED_EMAIL

WCB_CC_REJECTED_EMAIL

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process Navigator/ Solution Scenario/ mandatory Finance Best Practices
entry.content:
Dear SAP users, 

Which Finance Business Processes are mandatory in a greenfield implementation of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (version 2408) for an IT consulting company specializing in outsourcing consultants? The company provides professional services, primarily to the public sector, and aims to optimize its financial processes with this implementation. Considering the focus on project-based services and resource management, which financial processes do you believe are essential to include in the scope?

In SAP Signavio Process Navigator I see the following list: 

Accounting and Financial Close
-
Advanced Accounting and Financial Close
-
Advanced Financial Operations
-
Cost Management and Profitability Analysis
-
Enterprise Risk and Compliance
-
Environmental Footprint Management
-
Financial Operations
-
Real Estate Management
-
Subscription Billing and Revenue Management
-
Treasury Management
-

 

 

However, not all are mandatory to implement i guess. 

 

Which FICO expert can advise on this?

entry.title: Where can I learn mor about change management methodology an activities?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Acting as Professional Change Manager in SAP Cloud Projects learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey will enable the learner to apply organizational change management in an SAP cloud project to support the transformation process towards a high and sustainable user adoption and an accelerated adoption of innovation.

Learning objectives
After the completion of this learning journey, project managers and consultants will have developed an in-depth understanding of SAP’s organizational change management methodology. They will be able to plan and execute change management activities to professionally handle the people side of cloud deployments during all SAP Activate phases. In addition, they will be aware of the success factors for an effective change management delivery.

Prerequisites

  • Basic knowledge of project management, preferably in the context of SAP implementation projects.
  • Completion of the learning journey
    "Discover Organizational Change Management for SAP Cloud Projects".

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Start learning to develop extensions for SAP Business One, Web client for customer implementatio
entry.content:
Hi @HectorSantos 
Thanks for highlighting this: the link is changed and points now to the correct learning journey.
@inga_babco2 Thanks for sharing the link.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: PPDS - resource still showing after deletion
entry.content:
Hello,

I currently have the problem that a PPDS resource that i deleted is still showing up in the Fiori apps. As you can see here when searching in Location DE02 for a resource, it says there arent any

But when i go into the Fiori Apps "Production Scheduling Board" or "Monitor Capactiy Utilization" one resource still shows up.

entry.title: Re: Create Virtual Table in HDI
entry.content: I have a requirement where I have to create virtual tables to bring s4 hana data to cloud and then build views and expose them as a services . Views and services should be built on capm strictly using the virtual tables created. I have no idea about this, Can anyone explain how we can achieve this. I went through this blog but it mentions nothing about how to use the virtual tables create and create views and services on top of that.

entry.title: Re: Missing privileges to publish the changes on Budget v...
entry.content: Sharing the model to user with maintain right allows user to plan on the model in general, it's required even if there is no DAC dimension in the model. In addition, DAC allow admin to control which data region of model is user allowed to plan on. They work together.

entry.title: Re: Import Sales Orders (Part 9): Creation with Reference
entry.content:
Hello,

How to force an empty field in the template?

For example, we have Delivery Block 01 by the default in the system. We want to have all uploaded Sales Order to have Delivery Block file empty. If we add HEADER.DELIVERYBLOCKREASON an have an empty value - it will be ingnored and default Block reason will be populated by the system.

 

Thank you!

entry.title: "Historical Status is invalid" message while generating PDS for iPPE Structure with Change Number
entry.content:
Hi,

   We are Testing ECM in iPPE with parameter effectivity, we have maintained a change number for the structure nodes in product structure. While generating PDS for iPPE structure with ECM (Parameter effectivity) , system through message for Structure node "historical status is invalid" in log.

If we used the same iPPE structure with ECM without Parameter Effectivity, PDS got generated successfully. But when we create change number with parameter effectivity, we are getting "Historical status is not valid".

entry.title: Re: Maintain username and password for 3rd party MQTT broker SAP DM
entry.content: @Manoel can you help on this one?

entry.title: Unicode Conversion Issue - ABAP, modification in database table.
entry.content:
Facing unicode conversion issue in modify statement.

 

Here's the code

DATA: datum TYPE sy-datum.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
PARAMETERS: p_date TYPE datum.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

CONSTANTS:lc_tabname TYPE tabname VALUE 'ztfre_layout'.

IF p_date IS INITIAL.
p_date = sy-datum - 1.
ENDIF.

IF p_date IS NOT INITIAL.

SELECT min_stock,
zvalto
FROM ztfre_layout
WHERE ( min_stock IS NOT INITIAL AND zvalto LT @p_date )
OR min_stock IS INITIAL
INTO TABLE @DATA(layout_tab).
IF sy-subrc = 0.

LOOP AT layout_tab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<layout>).
<layout>-min_stock = 0.
ENDLOOP.


IF layout_tab IS NOT INITIAL.

CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_E_TABLE'
EXPORTING
mode_rstable = 'E'
tabname = lc_tabname
EXCEPTIONS
foreign_lock = 1
system_failure = 2
OTHERS = 3.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
MODIFY ztfre_layout FROM TABLE layout_tab.
MESSAGE: 'Records updated successfully' TYPE 'S'.
COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.

CALL FUNCTION 'DEQUEUE_E_TABLE'
EXPORTING
tabname = lc_tabname.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

ENDIF.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Automatic Pop up of batch characteristic window while doing goods receipt in Inbound delivery.
entry.content:
Hello Sapiens,

 i am testing Batch management in SAP Embedded EWM. I did configuration to create Batch automatically while creating Inbound delivery. i also made the entry of Characteristics of the Batch mandatory. As the entry of Characteristics of Batch made mandatory the system should not allow the Goods receipt untill Characteristics are maintained for that Batch. But in my case the goods receipt is accepted without maintaining Characteristics. 

My requirement : System should not accept goods receipt untill the characteristics are maintained for the Batch. When i click on Goods receipt in /SCWM/PRDI  system should pop up the characteristics screen (MSC2N). 

I have seen an video related to Batch management and in this Video at Timing 6 mins 50 second has the same requirement as i mentioned above. Following is the video Link.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v80BXtiPNBY

I had searched for  BADI to implement to meet my requirements. But i did not found any BADI. 

Are there  any BADI or User exists ?

Are they any other configuration i am missing, to be done to meet my requirement?

Please do you have an idea, technical or functional to obtain this result?

I really appreciate your help.

Thank you

Regards,
VG REDDY

entry.title: SAP Developer News, November 14th, 2024
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Developing Event-Driven Integrations with SAP BTP 📖

  • SAP Press E-Bite: https://www.sap-press.com/developing-event-driven-integrations-with-sap-btp_6021/
  • GitHub repository: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-e-bite/

Terraform Provider Updates

  • Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/may-i-introduce-you-terraform-exporter-sap-btp-christian-lechner-lgzif/
  • Terraform Provider for Cloud Foundry 1.1.0 https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/terraform-provider-cloud-foundry-v110-whats-new-debaditya-ray-xyxyc/

Report from SAP Insider EMEA 2024

  • SAP Insider EMEA 2024 event https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024
  • UKISUG Connect 2024 event https://www.ukisugconnect.co.uk/
  • SAUG-x event https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024

Report from ASUG Tech Connect

  • https://www.asug.com/events/asug-tech-connect-2024

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

00:07 Report from ASUG Tech Connect

02:47 Report from SAP Insider EMEA 2024

04:10 Developing Event-Driven Integrations with SAP BTP 📖

05:04 Terraform Provider Updates

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for November 14th, 2024.

[Devtoberfest Winners] Hi, I'm Bato. I'm one of the Devtoberfest winners of last year. I'm here at the ASUG and enjoying a conference, doing hands-on labs, doing interesting sessions from SAP and some partners and enjoying the time. Hi, my name is Sebastian. I'm coming from Germany and I'm one of the Devtoberfest winner from 2023. It's my pleasure to be here. It's very nice TechEd conference and I love it to contact a lot of other people and to have fun. My name is Daniel also winner of this year's Devtoberfest. I'm very excited to be here. We learned a lot of new things and I'm also very excited for the disney film tomorrow. Hello, my name is Jarosław Piotrko, I won the Devtoberfest 2023. I would like to greet all programmers from Poland and encourage you to participate in Devtoberfest, because you always have a chance to win and participate in such a fantastic event, and in the journey to the Wolt Adysteia Park. Thank you. Hello, I am Abul Eshtam, the winner of Devtoberfest 2023. Here I am at SAP TechConnect attending this session on AI and different solutions of SAP and about knowing about SAP Joule. A big thanks to SAP Developer Advocate for this opportunity. Thank you. Hi, I am Sandra, I am from Mexico, and I am a winner of Devtoberfest  2023. So, hi developers, I am here in Teques, finally I met the developer advocates and I have learned a lot on the hands-on sessions. See you! Hola, yo soy Sandra de Mexico y soy una de las ganadoras del Devtoberfest 2023. Hi Bablock, I'm finally in SAP TechEd and I met the developers advocates and I've also learned a lot about hands-on. I'll tell you later. See you!

[DJ] Good morning, I'm here in Copenhagen, day three, the morning of day three, the last day of the SAP Insider EMEA 2024 event. As you can see, there's a little bit of a great time happening right now. I'm here in the LEGO area, what's not to like, but basically, it's been a great event. This is one of a number of events that's been happening post SAP TechEd Virtual 2024. We've had three days, well, two and a half days so far, all fantastic conversation, face-to-face conversation, talking about tech, getting to know each other, and also learning from each other in various sessions, lectures, but also hands-on sessions, two-hour hands-on sessions that have been very successful and very well received. We've also had a developer garage staffed by the developer advocates just down there. But anyway, it's been a great event. Look out for more events coming your way. There's one in Birmingham in the UK in early December as part of the UKISUG Connect and also one in Australia coming up next week, I think. Anyway, it's me, DJ, signing off for now from SAP Insider EMEA 2024. Cool.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Are you interested in learning about event-driven integrations? Well, I've got some news to share with you. Check out the new SAP Press eByte Developing Event-Driven Integrations with SAP BTP. It was released this week. Event-driven integrations are all about systems communicating in real time, reacting to changes instantly, and creating seamless experience for our customers. In the invite, I dive deep into how you can leverage SAP BTP to build event-driven integrations with the different offerings available. There is also an accompanying GitHub repo with code samples and integration artifacts to help you get started. It is a great time to explore how event-driven architectures can transform your integration strategy. I'm including the links in the description. Enjoy.

[DJ] In the infrastructures code arena, Terraform is where it's at and the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP released last year is already a really successful piece of software. This week a new part of the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP was released, the Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP. Now this is a result of lots of customer feedback, the number one wish is to of course be able to start with existing resources on SAP BTP and bring them into the Terraform home. This is a beta release of the Terraform exporter for SAP BTP, there's plenty of information about it from Christian's blog post, the documentation on the GitHub repo and there's also a video. So why not give it a try and let the team know what you think, they're eager to find out. And talking of Terraform, the latest release of the newly written Cloud Foundry provider, the Terraform provider for Cloud Foundry is out 1.1.0. This release brings lots of new features and improvements, so check out the blog post and give that a try as well. Just if you're wondering, just as the BTP CLI is for managing resources on SAP BTP on the command line, and the CF CLI is for managing resources in Cloud Foundry specifically on the command line, so is the Terraform provider for SAP PTP and the Terraform provider for Cloud Foundry. They sort of parallel each other. Anyway, give it a go, and again, let the team know what you think.

So I just selected the models in import options. But the import fails with the following messages (I also tried to select the space + the analytic models the second time):

 

Thanks in advance

Regards

Maike

entry.title: Re: How end to end point to point integrations work for GL Documents from SAP to SAP using SOAMANAGE
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions. Hopefully someone will fix it for you.

entry.title: Re: Error Message no. CJ055 'PEP element is already imputed and cannot be deleted'
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions. Hopefully someone will fix it for you.

entry.title: Concept for Warehouse Billing in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All

I am going to explain warehouse billing Concept in EWM.

Introduction- In EWM, we can record the quantity of warehouse services used for the services agreed upon in an agreement in the Transportation Management. We can subsequently send this quantity information back to the TM for charge calculation and settlement based on the charges you have agreed to in the agreement

Benefits-

1- Sell warehouse services to customers and bill the customers periodically based the services used for a time period

2- Purchase warehouse services from external service providers and periodically pay the service provider based on the warehouse services used for a time period.

Available Features in Warehouse Billing

1- Snapshot management
2- Warehouse billing measurement request management
3- Warehouse billing measurement management

We will describe each of one 

1- Snapshot Management - We use this feature to capture the EWM business data relevant to measuring warehouse services and for calculating the charges for billing

We can record below mentioned business data in snapshot management

1- Snapshot of Physical Stock - We create a snapshot for stock at a particular time. The system records the warehouse inventory data at this particular time. We can create snapshots to record the presence of physical stock on a daily basis. If We create multiple snapshots in one day, the system merges the physical stock on that specific date.

2- Snapshot for Inbound Deliveries - The system records business data for goods that have been received and confirmed in Inbound Delivery Document (Document Type PDI). If the inbound delivery items are partially received, the item is recorded partially with the quantity

3- Snapshot for Outbound Deliveries-The system records business data for goods that have been sent and have a completed status in Outbound Delivery Document (Document Type FDO).

4- Snapshot for Warehouse tasks-The system records data for warehouse tasks that have been carried out and confirmed

We below Scenarios to perform 

 1- We can Create Snapshots to create snapshots on a daily basis
2- Configure Snapshots to activate/deactivate, and specify other settings for the snapshots
3- To create snapshots using specify attributes, we can create snapshots for Physical stock, Inbound               deliveries, Outbound deliveries and warehouse tasks.

We can delete and remove snapshots

2- Request for Warehouse Billing Measurement- EWM automatically creates a request for warehouse billing measurement (WBMR) from the Transportation Management (TM) agreement data. In the WBMR, we can assign warehouse billing measurement services to measure the warehouse services used, and periodically create warehouse billing measurements (WBMs) for calculating the quantity of services used for a billing period. The system uses the WBM information for calculating charges for the warehouse services used in the TM. When agreement information in TM is changed, the WBMR reflects the changes in the EWM.

WBMR Status

In Process
Activated
Deactivated

When you save and release an agreement in TM, EWM automatically receives the agreement as a request for warehouse billing measurement (WBMR). The WBMR has the same billing schedule determined in the agreement in TM. The billing schedule refers to a specified factory calendar both in TM and EWM. You must specify the same factory calendar settings in both the systems.

Warehouse Billing Measurement Service Assignment 

We can calculate the quantity of warehouse services used after you assign a warehouse billing measurement service to an item in a request for warehouse billing measurement document. We can measure the service quantity for the following EWM documents
Number of documents

Product quantity

Weight

Volume

Number of bins

Number of handling units

We can also specify a variant along with the warehouse billing measurement service in the WBMR item to enter specific constraints to measure warehouse services. For example, we can constrain the measurement to a specific group of documents, products, bins, or handling unit.

Note-The system subsequently measures the quantity from EWM business data in snapshots

3- Warehouse Billing Measurement Management-

After we have activated a request for billing measurement, we can display the generated warehouse billing measurements (WBM) in the Warehouse Management Monitor under Billing Warehouse Billing Measurement. The WBM contains the quantity of warehouse services used. A WBM can be generated for every billing period we have defined. The quantity in the WBM is obtained from the business data recorded into the snapshots. In the Warehouse Management Monitor, you can see the business data that was used to calculate the quantity as the proof of services.

Billing measurement document has following statuses

Ready for Distribution
Distributed
No Distribution

Reporting for Warehouse Billing in EWM Warehouse Monitor

For reporing we can use warehouse management monitor-Billing-Snapshots
For reporting we can use warehouse management monitor - Billing - Request for Warehouse Billing Measurement.

For reporing we can use warehouse management monitor-Billing-Billing Measurement

 

Please share your feedback to improve it 

 

Reference-https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/5ad63855b04c6a38e10000000a174cb4.html

 

 

Regerads

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - Usage of WSDLs in the SOAP Adapter
entry.content:
Hello Mandy

Yes, I agree to its better to have  instance of "integration-flow" plan  (used to post data to the iflow) to access the wsdl. Please check if that is possible to implement in the upcoming upgrades.

entry.title: Planned order is getting failed in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I'm trying to configure Repetitive Manufacturing integrating with SAP DM and configured all the settings mentioned in Integration guide. Unable to send planned order automatically created from MD11 as no IDoc is triggering but able to send the planned via POIT transaction.

But in DM, getting this below error:

"The bill of operations has no valid operations or phases"

below is the payload:

{
"orderDownloadedEventPayload": {
"orderAction": "CREATING",
"industryCategory": "DISCRETE",
"orderCategory": "PLANNED_ORDER",
"sourceSystemType": "ON_PREM",
"shopOrder": {
"orderNumber": "5145",
"plant": "2000",
"releaseStatus": "PARTIALLY_CREATED",
"executionStatus": "NOT_IN_EXECUTION",
"buildQuantity": {
"value": 1,
"isoCode": "PCE",
"internalCode": "ST"
},
"orderedQuantity": {
"value": 1,
"isoCode": "PCE",
"internalCode": "ST"
},
"productionQuantity": {
"value": 1,
"isoCode": "PCE",
"internalCode": "ST"
},
"releasedQuantity": null,
"materialId": {
"ref": "ItemBO:2000,FG_REM_CELL,ERP001",
"plant": "2000",
"material": "FG_REM_CELL",
"version": "ERP001"
},
"actualRouting": null,
"actualBom": null,
"shopOrderType": {
"ref": "ShopOrderTypeBO:2000,REPETITIVE",
"plant": "2000",
"orderType": "REPETITIVE"
},
"erpOrder": true,
"priority": 500,
"serializedSfcPatternKey": null,
"plannedStartDate": "2024-11-15T06:00:00.000+00:00",
"scheduledStartDate": "2024-11-15T22:37:30.000+00:00",
"shopFloorScheduledStartDate": null,
"plannedCompletionDate": "2024-11-16T05:59:59.000+00:00",
"scheduledCompletionDate": "2024-11-15T23:00:00.000+00:00",
"shopFloorScheduledCompletionDate": null,
"erpPutawayStorageLocation": "200B",
"batchNumber": "",
"longText": null,
"toleranceUnder": null,
"toleranceOver": null,
"overDeliveryIsAllowed": null,
"productionVersion": "001",
"inspectionLot": null,
"warehouseNumber": null,
"billOfMaterialVariantUsage": "1",
"billOfMaterialVariant": "1",
"billOfMaterial": "",
"billOfOperationsType": "R",
"billOfOperationsGroup": "50000005",
"billOfOperationsVariant": "1",
"erpCustomer": null,
"erpCustomerOrder": {
"customerOrder": "",
"customerOrderItem": "000000"
},
"erpOrderStatus": null,
"schedulingType": null,
"customValues": [],
"quantityValidation": null,
"quantityToRelease": null,
"bomHeaderCustomValues": [],
"relaxedFlow": null,
"orderCategory": "PLANNED_ORDER",
"isUpdated": false
},
"changeProduction": null,
"retainSchedules": false,
"activityNetworkElements": [],
"activityRelationships": [],
"orderItems": [],
"workInstructions": [],
"context": {
"ORDER_PROCESSING_MODE": "S0"
}
}
}

 

so please help me out, how to send planned order automatically and what does the meaning of the error in Integration message dashboard in SAP DM.

 

Regards,

Rahaman

entry.title: Re: Download com.sap.it.api.mapping.* jar file

entry.title: Re: BusinessObject DEV CMS DB deleted .How to refresh it...
entry.content: I don't think the version of the client would cause that problem. For a next step I'd suggest checking the trace files for the SIA and CMS.

entry.title: Re: Integrating Onboarding and Learning For New Hires Before Day-1
entry.content: Awesome work Sachin!

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer News, November 14th, 2024
entry.content:
Hi ,

Great update on the latest developments! The event-driven integrations with SAP BTP are a game-changer for real-time system communication. I’m excited to dive into the SAP Press e-Bite and the accompanying GitHub repo for hands-on examples—this is a great resource for building seamless integrations.

Also, the Terraform Exporter for SAP BTP is a fantastic tool for managing existing resources in Terraform. It’s great to see Cloud Foundry and SAP BTP resources being integrated more easily into infrastructure-as-code workflows.

Looking forward to exploring these updates further and engaging with the community at events like SAP Insider EMEA and ASUG Tech Connect!

Best,

Azmath.

entry.title: Re: How to get the error details in SAP CPI when calling...
entry.content:
Hi ,

// Retrieve the exception and MPL ID (if available)
def exceptionMessage = exception.getMessage()
def mplId = message.getHeader("MPL_ID")

// Prepare the custom error message
def errorMessage = "An internal server error occurred: MPL ID: ${mplId}, Error Message: ${exceptionMessage}"

// Set the custom error message in the response body
message.setBody(errorMessage)

// Set a custom HTTP status code (for example 400 instead of 500)
message.setHeader("HTTP_Response_Code", "400")

// Log the error (optional, for debugging)
messageLogFactory.getLogger().error("Custom Error: ${errorMessage}")

Regards,

entry.title: Re: How to lock the CO period and plan version in the pub...
entry.content:
Dear @Chanaporn,

in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition SAP Analytics Cloud is the primary planning tool, complete financial planning is done in SAP Analytics Cloud.
This is the reason why there is no version plan for selecting the lock period.

Please refer to the our Integrated Financial Planning - An Overview blog, which explains how planning is done in SAP Analytics Cloud and provides links to additional information.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD EDITION - CDS_VIEW FOR TAX RATE INFORMATION NOT FOUND
entry.content:
Good Morning,

We are trying to get in S4HANA PUBLIC EDITION  tax rate amount in cds views but we are not able to get the proper one. Neither in other blogs , community and post around.

Best regards,

Guillermo

entry.title: Is it possible that spread square frame of item of apps "Post General Entries" ?
entry.content:
Dear All,

Is it possible that spread square frame of item of apps "Post General Entries" ?

This is the red line in the image.

If it cannot be done without UI Adaptation support, how do I search for apps that UI Adaptation supports?

Best Regards,

Yuki Tsumura

entry.title: RFEBKA00 not clearing Documents
entry.content:
We were originally on ECC and upgrade to S4 1 - 2 years ago.  Everything is working fine with RFEBKA00 and clearing documents properly with Banks that were set up before the S4 conversion. 

Now we have added a new bank.  We copied an existing pre processing program that calls RFEBKA00, because we needed to remove the leading zeros from the checks.  When running this new program,  the checks are marked as cashed, but not clearing and making the entry.  

Because we are using the same variant in RFEBKA00 as other banks that are working, I am thinking that end should be configured correctly.  

TCode OT83 is created consistent with the other banks, and FBZP looks consistent with the Account Numbers. 

I am wondering if there is another piece of configuration (may different between ECC and S4) that is missing.  

Thanks in advance for any guidance you can provide.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Voice: Interesting Posts, New Areas, More Events!
entry.content: Nice work! Congratulations!

entry.title: Re: Search help restrictions fields are disabled for input.
entry.content:
Sdis" field, when checked the parameter is displayed but cannot be changed

"Display field in dialog box of an elementary search help If this flag is set, the search help parameter appears in the selection popup window for restricting values as a pure display field. The contents of the parameter inform the user about the restriction, but he cannot change it. This is advisable for example when the parameter is an import parameter or if it has a default value."

entry.title: SAC Optimized Story - Export to Csv / Xlsx - Script Error
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working with an Optimized Story in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) where I have created a button with a script to export table data to a CSV/Xlsx file. When I attempt to export the table using the button in our current tenant, I receive the following error message:

However, when I try the exact same export in another tenant, it works without any issues. Both tenants are using import data. Does anyone know the possible reason for this discrepancy?

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Search help restrictions fields are disabled for input.
entry.content: Thanks. Sorry, I forgot that!

entry.title: Territory Deletion / Realignment of history data (C4C Sales V1)
entry.content:
Hello, I deleted a territory after a realignment run, because I thought history data would be realigned, too. It hasn't. I can see the territory when clicking on history data, but I can't do any changes and the sales responsible lost authorization on all historical quotations/opportunities. This is really a big problem.

Can a deleted territory be reactivated?

How can I change the territory in history data (quotations/opp)? Field is blocked for manual changes.

Many thanks for any help!

Kind regards

Sandra

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Good morning,

Applied Note 3518324 - TR 941 form Quarter Check Box not flagged for 3rd and 4th quarter, after the note was applied, ran PU19 for the 941. 3rd quarter the check box is not marked but 4th quarter is marked.  Do you know if there is another note for this?

entry.title: I am a consultant. Where can I start learning how the basic procurement process works?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing Procurement Processes learning journey.


Overview
This course is a beginner course designed to give consultants who are completely new to the procurement area an overview of how the basic procurement process generally works. These could, for example, be consultants who have previously worked in other areas and are now being used in the procurement process. They should then be able to distinguish the processes between SAP S/4 HANA and SAP Ariba and be able to identify where there is potential for integration between the two solutions.

Learning objectives
At the end of this course, the learner will be able to:

  • Demonstrate the fundamental procurement principles, practices, and processes, enabling them to navigate the procurement lifecycle effectively.
  • Develop the skills necessary to analyze procurement requirements, select appropriate procurement methods, and manage supplier relationships efficiently within diverse organizational contexts.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Check the related Live Sessions and engage and learn with SAP experts and peers.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Production Scheduling Board (F2176)
entry.content: I have tried to find a way for the Fiori capacity planning board or table to take setup times into account depending on existing setup matrices. I currently see no way to take these dynamic setup times into account in the new Fiori apps for capacity planning. Has anyone found a way to take setup times from setup transitions into account here?

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Capacity Requirement planning in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
I have tried to find a way for the Fiori capacity planning board or table to take setup times into account depending on existing setup matrices (since in its predecessor CM25 this is possible).
I currently see no way to take these dynamic setup times into account in the new Fiori apps for capacity planning.

Has anyone found a way to take setup times from setup transitions into account here?

entry.title: Re: FIORI app for CM25/CM21 capacity leveling
entry.content: I have tried to find a way for the Fiori capacity planning board or table to take setup times into account depending on existing setup matrices. I currently see no way to take these dynamic setup times into account in the new Fiori apps for capacity planning. Has anyone found a way to take setup times from setup transitions into account here?

entry.title: Need urgent help!
entry.content:
Hello! I am seasoned PD/PLM leader & urgently looking job in SAP domain, (H1B Visa) in the ‘Enterprise Product Development & management' with over 15 years of excellence in the automotive ecosystem to drive growth! I offer end-to-end support to ‘New Product Dev’ (PD/SDLC & PLM) from market research, configuration management, concept design optimization, Project Management, Supply chain to go2market launch!

Skill sets; Product Leader & Solutions Architect | SAP Digital Manufacturing, ECTR, S4HANA | MBSE Integrations: 3DEXPERIENCE, TeamCenter | SQL, Java, Python | Functional Safety | Student- MS.PD (INCOSE Cert, Systems Engg. Dec25)

I am available on C2C immediately within a week. & willing to relocate across US. I’m excited to add values to your team!

Please contact me: hoshing.abhijeet@gmail.com or on my cell# (734)787-9539

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to check the TLS Version
entry.content:
How can we verify the current TLS version? Is a version update required?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check the TLS Version
entry.content:
To check the SSL version being used you can use a tool like SSL Labs' SSL Test. Here’s how you can do it:

  1. Go to the SSL Labs SSL Test tool. (https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/)
  2. Enter the URL into the provided field.
  3. The tool will analyze and provide detailed information about the supported SSL versions and cipher suites.


Cipher Suites used are only TLS: 1.2 and 1.3, there is no lower version TLS used by default in SAP Commerce Cloud. For more information, see Trusted TLS/SSL Ciphers.

entry.title: Hello!
entry.content:
Greetings to you all over the world!

My name is Frank. New to the community but an SAP user for 20 years. Still a lot to learn. 😉

Happy to be here & see you,

Frank

entry.title: Re: ABAP Core Development Tools 3.42.0 vs ABAP Core Devel...
entry.content:
Dear Ingo,

First of all thank you for your guidance. You were right, the issue was at my side. I am now describing what I have done in case it can help anyone else.

First of all, apologies for a subject misleading

The wizard in ABAP Core Development Tools 3.46.0 is different than the ABAP Core Development Tools 3.42.0 but it has nothing to do with the root-cause.

The important here is the error messaged that I received before solving the issue:

"

Failed to get service instance URL info for

 https: // XXXXX.abap.us10.hana.ondemand.com: java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException: org.apache.hc.client5.http.ClientProtocolException: CONNECT refused by proxy: HTTP/1.1 407 Proxy"

The important part here is the last 2 words: 407 which means authorization and proxy. In other words your proxy is blocking the access to the SAP BTP trial account

Standard solution would be to go to Eclipse > Menu > Window > Preferences > General > Network connections and write the IP of your proxy and the password.

I am trying to connect with my company notebook which has PROXY centrally managed, so I need to create a ticket at my company and they solve it. You can close now this question and mark it as solved.

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion in People Story reports
entry.content:
Hi @marianolanza 

thank you, I've this table.

Regards,

Aneta

entry.title: Re: SAP analytics cloud - How can i filter a story based...
entry.content:
Dear Venkat,

Thanks a lot for your response. some how your solution almost achieved what I need. I have created a model from an excel (userID and departmentName) as columns and gave them like two dummy rows. however, im getting this error

entry.title: Re: MESSAGE Unable to resolve plug-in "com.sap.adt.ddic.t...
entry.content:
First of all thank you for the hints to the community and apologies for a subject misleading:

I took the title, as I wrote below, from a log file that I found in Eclipse, but it is not relevant nor related to the root-cause

The root-cause of the issue was at my side. I am now describing what I have done in case it can help anyone else.

 

The important here is the error message that I received before solving the issue:

"

Failed to get service instance URL info for

 https: // XXXXX.abap.us10.hana.ondemand.com: java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException: org.apache.hc.client5.http.ClientProtocolException: CONNECT refused by proxy: HTTP/1.1 407 Proxy"

The important part here is the last 2 words: 407 which means authorization and proxy. In other words your proxy is blocking the access to the SAP BTP trial account

Standard solution would be to go to Eclipse > Menu > Window > Preferences > General > Network connections and write the IP of your proxy and the password.

I was trying to connect with my company notebook which has PROXY centrally managed, so I needed to create a ticket at my company and they solve it. You can close now this question and mark it as solved.

entry.title: Re: "SAP Integration Suite application is not subscribed" error in BTP trial account
entry.content: I am getting the same error.

entry.title: Loading Time by Crane to be displayed on Order confirmation and Delivery confirmation (outbound)
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have one requirement received as below:

Customer wants loading done by Crane for material  to be included exactly with Time and agreed Rate on delivery note copy and order confirmation.

Currently on delivery note it is appeared as below:

Material No                      QTY Unit      Unit price     Unit       Net price USD
AB23000BW                    10 TO(ton)       35              1 TO      350
special Crane of 40M

Customer suggests  now as below. The query is how can we achieve this technically without ABAP code :

The suggestion would now be that the recorded tonnage is multiplied by a fixed value of 10 minutes and displayed either as text or as an additional item number, e.g.: “The included unloading time is XX minutes” or as a separate item number “Included unloading time".

Item number also they need to be appeared automatically as soon as they select "Crane loading" in Delivery creation.

Thank you for the help.

Regards

SAPSCM

entry.title: Re: ME21N CHECK ON WARNING RED-LIGHTS NEEDS TO STOP THE P...
entry.content:
First correct your implementing class of ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST

  • Use provided macros to raise error message
  • In case of error invalidate the item in error

Sample for error handling

 

 

          mmpur_metafield mmmfd_cust_01.
          MESSAGE e083(me) WITH text-004 space INTO lv_dummy.
          mmpur_message_forced sy-msgty sy-msgid sy-msgno
                               sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
          CALL METHOD im_item->invalidate( ).

 

 

There's another BAdI for disabling hold/parking options: ME_HOLD_PO

  • But first try disabling faulty elements, especially in any CHECK method loop
  • You don't want an angry user to lose his 1000 items input cause one item is in error, just before week-end?

entry.title: Inner join with AND , OR and LIKE in where clause select query
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

               When I am using the multiple OR clauses in where condition for individual tables it is working fine but when using the multiple OR  clauses with inner join in where clause it is only considering the first condition. 

The following is the code which helps you understand better.

 

    if lt_bsad is not initial.
      select rbukrs,
             gjahr,
             belnr,
             wsl,
             augbl,
             gkont from acdoca into table @LT_acdoca
        for all entries in @LT_bsad where rbukrs = @LT_bsad-bukrs and
                                          gjahr  = @LT_bsad-gjahr and
                                          augbl  = @LT_bsad-augbl and
                                          rldnr  = '0L'.

    endif.

    if lt_acdoca is not initial.
      select spras,
             saknr,
             txt50 from skat into table @DATA(lt_skat)
        for all entries in @LT_acdoca where saknr = @LT_acdoca-gkont and
                                            txt50 like '%TDS%' or
                                            txt50 like '%CASH DISCOUNT%' or
                                            txt50 like '%GBI%' or
                                            txt50 like '%CUF%' and
                                            spras = 'E'.
    endif.

 The above code is picking 6 records each in lt_acdoca and lt_skat which is absolutely correct. 
But I want to get the inner join of both acdoca and skat so that I can pick the acdoca-wsl from acdoca to pass it in final internal table based on txt50 as shown above.
The below is the select query with inner join of the above two tables.

    select acdoca~rbukrs,
           acdoca~gjahr,
           acdoca~belnr,
           acdoca~wsl,
           acdoca~augbl,
           acdoca~gkont,
           skat~spras,
           skat~saknr,
           skat~txt50
         from acdoca as acdoca inner join skat as skat
     on skat~saknr = acdoca~gkont
      into table @DATA(lt_acdoca_skat)
    for all entries in @LT_bsad where  acdoca~rbukrs = @LT_bsad-bukrs and
                                        acdoca~gjahr  = @LT_bsad-gjahr and
                                        acdoca~augbl  = @LT_bsad-augbl and
                                        acdoca~rldnr  = '0L' and
                                        skat~spras = 'E' and
                                        ( skat~txt50 like '%TDS%' or
                                          skat~txt50 like '%CASH DISCOUNT%' 
                                                                     or
                                         skat~txt50 like '%GBI%' or
                                          skat~txt50 like '%CUF%'
                                         ).
*

Now it is giving the result with 'TDS' only . But I want the lt_acdoca_skat with 'GBI' , 'CUF', 'CASH DISCOUNT' also.

The below is the image of 6 records in lt_skat

The below is the one record with the TDS. So it is taking the first condition of TDS and skipping the others.

I want the lt_acdoca_skat with all 6 records of lt_acdoca and lt_skat .

How can I achieve it  ?

entry.title: Re: Analytical CDS Views - union associations is differen...

don't know how you checked, the _user association is clearly different.

basically you have to make the cds you are trying to union to be "same"

entry.content:
Hi everybody,

When we tried to delete a PEP element in our Project and the next error shows up:

'PEP element is already imputed and cannot be deleted' Nº CJ055

Our project and PEP elements are only in 'Created' status.

Any solutions ?

Regards Community

entry.title: How can the documents listed in the report 'RFDOPR20' be identified?
entry.content:
Dear Esteemed SAP Financial Experts,

For analyzing customer payment history, SAP ERP provides the report RFDOPR20 or the transaction code S_ALR_87012177. When this report is executed, the selection screen allows the selection of parameters such as a customer and a company code.

I would like to inquire about the documents listed in the field "Number of Incoming Payments during the Period" (technical name: ANZ01) for each period:

  • How can these documents be identified?
  • Which criteria (e.g., document type, company code, fiscal year, etc.) are necessary to accurately extract these payment receipts from tables such as BKPF, BSEG, BSAD, and others?

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,
Emal

entry.title: Re: Configure the SSO between IAS and SuccessFactors(SF) by SAML
entry.content:
Hi

I wonder why you're recommending this approach...There's a whole community and a bunch of docs from SAP providing guidance to migrate SF to IAS and this is done by a specific process in the SF Upgrade center, not manually like you're describing

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/migration-to-sap-cloud-identity-authentication-ias-ips-innovation-alert-amp/ba-p/271407

entry.title: Materialize PAL CALL RESULT
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have a technical topic I cannot overcome within PAL.

Let's say that I have written a routine where I want to call a PAL function, SAP HANA

Is there a possibility to materialize the result into a table? 

I have tried several possibilities but I cannot overcome the issue.

Thank you,

Regards,

Nicola

 

 

 

SET SCHEMA DM_PAL;DROP TABLE PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL;CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL (
    "ID" INTEGER,
    "X1" DOUBLE,
    "X2" DOUBLE);INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (0, 6.0, 9.0) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (1, 12.1, 8.3) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (2, 13.5, 15.3) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (3, 15.4, 18.7) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (4, 10.2, 19.8) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (5, 23.3, 20.6) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (6, 24.4,24.3) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (7, 30.6, 25.3) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (8, 32.5, 27.6) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (9, 25.6, 28.5) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (10, 38.7, 29.4) ;INSERT INTO PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL VALUES (11, 38.7, 29.4) ;DROP TABLE #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL;CREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY COLUMN TABLE #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL (
    "PARAM_NAME" VARCHAR (256),
    "INT_VALUE" INTEGER,
    "DOUBLE_VALUE" DOUBLE,
    "STRING_VALUE" VARCHAR (1000)
);INSERT INTO #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('SCALING_METHOD',0,NULL, NULL);INSERT INTO #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('Z-SCORE_METHOD',0, NULL, NULL);INSERT INTO #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('NEW_MAX', NULL,1.0, NULL);INSERT INTO #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('NEW_MIN', NULL,0.0, NULL);CALL "_SYS_AFL"."PAL_SCALE"(PAL_SCALING_DATA_TBL, #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL, ?, ?, ?, ?);

entry.content: @Henry_Banks

entry.title: Re: Supply Assignment (ARun) in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content: Hello Ramesh - How we can change the batches in delivery. Currently its not allowing to change it in delivery as maybe its assigned to sales order. The scenario is CSR team creates the sales order and  assign batch according to customer specific shelf life requirement but once it goes warehouse, the team can pick batches other than mentioned in SO. How we can apply those changes in delivery. is it possible through supply assignment?

entry.title: Re: SAP analytics cloud - How can i filter a story based...
entry.content:
This is the simple csv model i used

and this is the script:

var loggeduserx = Application.getUserInfo().displayName;

Table_3.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("UserID",loggeduserx);

var tb3rslt =Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();

var deptName = tb3rslt[0]["DepartmentName"].id;
InputControl_2.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(deptName);

entry.title: Re: Issue with Guest User Provisioning to SAP IAS
entry.content:
Hi Ari

If you have enabled "create shadows users during logon" for the IDP named "IAS" i guess that what you see there on those "weird" users is just the automatic creation happending once a guest logins for the first time. The username might come from the "sub" or some of the claims from openID that has such format. The emails "xxxx@user.from.sap.custom.cf" are created when BTP is not able to resolve the mail attribute from IDP.

If you have configured the IDP named "IAS" with the standard option "establish trust" I'd have a look at the application created in IAS named "SAP BTP Subaccount xxxxx" and check the attribute mappings to make sure the attributes you're using exist for the Guest users.

entry.title: Re: Search help restrictions fields are disabled for input.
entry.content: Sandra: The search help is called in several places, including as a matchcode (F4) in a Selection screen parameter.

I’d love to hear more about any experiences with balancing these integrations for efficiency.

entry.content:
Hello community! I come to ask you for help with this error that I'm receiving when making a POST request to a service created with RAP where I have a child entity created as a composition that allows the loading of a document. In the implementation of the behavior I only have added the adjust number method for late numbering, nothing additional. I don't understand what the error is and how I can fix it.

The error occurs after the record has been correctly created in db.

entry.title: Dynamic Filter on Hierarchy 0YEA_MON_DAY
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a Bex query report that has as rows the Requisition (Request) Date with the enabled hierarchy (0YEA_MON_DAY) but when it runs in analysis or RSRT it shows no data as the values are outside of the hierarchy nodes. Meaning that the hierarchy is maintained until 2020 and by dynamic filtering it filters out the rows >2020 . How can i maintain this virtual hierarchy?

entry.title: ASUG Tech Connect SWAG - Dallas community
entry.content:
Hello Dallas group, 

hope you all had a good week. If you didn't get a chance to go to ASUG Tech Connect 2024 in WBP, I have a few swag items provided by the #SAPCommunity I will be giving out in our next #Dallas event

@rileyrainey @lingaiahvanam  @StephanieMarley @KatherineKenneally

entry.title: Re: ABAP Units test environment class CL_FUNCTION_TEST_ENVIRONMENT/If_FUNCTION_TEST_ENVIRONMENT does
entry.content:
The basic idea is to replace all external calls like the call of a Function module by mocks. So the productive code will call a class which implements the BAPI call in a method via an interface and the unit test calls a mock class sharing the same interface, does nothing, but returning the expected / desired value. Maybe a bit old but in my opinion still worth. 
Especially if you do not have the testdoubles in your system. 
Writing Testable Code for ABAP (sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Unicode Conversion Issue - ABAP, modification in database table.
entry.content:
Hi,

It's urgent, can anyone help.

entry.title: Re: Grouping all SAP TM Blogs which I have created into to this one to centralized it.
entry.content: Many thanks for that! Great content!

entry.title: Focussed Run - How to create a consolidated report of Guided Procedure Execution Output?
entry.content: Team - I have a guided procedure which is executed on several hosts. Is there an option by which i can download the output of this guided procedure execution for all systems?

entry.title: Re: Correlated Confirmation Feature SD(BOM)
entry.content:
Hi esatesenek, 

thanks a lot for the explanation. This is cool. But I have a question though. 
I have done all as described above, now I create an order, ordering 3pcs of the BOM material => explosion happens as expected, I get two components below the header material with 3pcs each. That is correct, as per may setup. 
Now I realize that I do need 4pcs, not 3pcs. So I change the qty on the BOM header material to 4 and hit enter again but nothing happens on the components. The components stay with 3pcs. Also, when I reduce from 3 down to 1 pcs the components stay as in the first explosion. Any idea how to fix this - do I need to manually adjust the qty on the components? 

Thanks in advance for your help and best regards

Bettina

entry.title: Re: Report on Work Location in Time Posting
entry.content:
Hi Rao,

The API https://api.sap.com/api/API_MANAGE_WORKFORCE_TIMESHEET/path/get_TimeSheetEntryCollection gives you the worklocation code in the timesheet. Likewise, the CDS view I_TimeSheetRecord provides you the work location code. You can build your own custom report via key user extensibility with this CDS view.

Regards,

Dennis

entry.title: Re: SAP analytics cloud - How can i filter a story based...
entry.content:
same error, I am getting this error specifically when i add this line:

var deptName = tb3rslt[0]["DepartmentName"].id; 

I am going to accept the original solution as it works with another model.

If you care and have free time, then you can help me with this issue and let me know what other ways can be done in SAC to achieve this.

Thank you

Dennis

Mostly those data come from table KNB4.

  • Look in forum for threads such as How table KNB4 is filled/calculated?
  • Look at OB41 Customizing for Sales Relevant Posting Keys (TBSL-XUMSW)

entry.title: Re: PDS transfer using curto create
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a PDS with PEO Materials (Shopfloor routing and a versioned BOM). As the curtoadv_create supports this feature i am still getting uncomplete PDS structure where as the activity and the components are missing. It seems that the plan type is still being routing where it should be shop floor routing. Can anyone help me further?

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Lock a Production Order from Changes by an External system?
entry.content:
We are integrating S4 with our MES.

We want to allow changes to the Production Order from the time is it released to the MES, up until the time an MES operator clicks a button that says, "I've started working on this Production Order."

Is there an API call or message that the MES can send to S4 to lock the production order from further changes?

entry.title: Re: How do we restrict the user assignments in SAP MDG Substitution? Do we have any enhancement opti
entry.content: Take a look at the document about rule-based workflow: https://www.sap.com/documents/2021/05/3e792b4b-e07d-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

entry.title: Re: Two Major News with SUM 2.0 SP 17
entry.content:
Hi Borris,

We are working with DMOVE2S4 approach for conversion to S/4HANA. 

I got a SAP ppt where it is showing that AAS must be installed on Migration Server pointing to source ECC system. I tried to simulate same approach but it is not working because it is not getting details of ASCS instance which is but obvious because /sapmnt is not shared between source ECC system and new server ( which is similar to migration server) so it will not detect presence of ASCS. However, it is connecting to DB of source ECC system and stopping/starting successfully. I have tried multiple SAP notes with no success. 

Your blog is mentioning that AAS must be installed on the HANA DB server pointing to source ECC system. 

Now question is SAP is not going to give us access on HANA DB server but will give full access on Migration Server. Then in that case what is the proposed solution in this situation. 

We are in between and loosing the timeline of the project. Request your quick response. 

Best Regards,

Sandeep

entry.title: AI@FRE (PART 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails
entry.content:
### AI@FRE Blog Series:

AI@FRE (Part 1) - Enabling Enterprise AI Adoption through the BTP FRE Engagement Model

AI@FRE (Part 2) - Accelerating Business AI Transformation with the FRE Value Framework

AI@FRE (Part 3) – Guardrails for Enterprise AI Adoption

AI@FRE (PART 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails 

###

Scaling enterprise AI is no longer optional; it's essential for organizations looking to remain competitive. However, achieving this requires robust architecture and tool standardization to enable scalable, secure, and efficient AI deployment. Standardizing key areas in an organization, from security to automation, digital experiences, core, data, and AI architecture, sets a strong foundation for streamlined processes, enhanced agility, and a simplified landscape. 

The Importance of Standardizing Architecture and Tools for AI at Scale 

Building a scalable AI infrastructure hinges on standardizing architecture and tools, which enables enterprises to measure key outcomes like: 

  • Business Agility & Flexibility: Achieve XX-month reduction in time-to-market for new AI initiatives. 
  • Execution Speed: Drive XX-day reduction in project execution. 
  • Simplified Landscape: Foster streamlined architecture that minimizes redundancies and supports easy upgrades and scalability. 

Each area of standardization brings unique benefits and helps ensure consistent integration across SAP solutions, as we’ll explore below. 

 

  1. Standardize Security Architecture 

With AI adoption, ensuring security at every layer of the architecture is critical. A standardized security architecture enables cohesive, scalable, and adaptive access management. 

  • User Access Management (IPS): Enforces access control, minimizing security risks by managing permissions and restricting access to sensitive data. 
  • Single Sign-On (IAS): Unifies login experiences across SAP solutions, enabling employees to access tools seamlessly. 
  • Automated, Adaptive Access Management: Automates access protocols to ensure compliance, adaptive access control, and lifecycle management. 
  • Event-Driven Identity Lifecycle Management: Automates identity management processes in response to organizational events, streamlining updates to user access and permissions. 

Business Benefits: 

  • Unified Access: Simplifies security management by centralizing access across SAP environments. 
  • Secure Remote Work: Provides reliable access for remote employees without compromising security. 
  • Enhanced Employee Experience: Eliminates repetitive logins, allowing employees to focus on tasks. 
  • Productivity and Collaboration: Reduces friction in accessing necessary tools, fostering productivity. 
  • Digital Transformation: Lays the security groundwork for digital transformation efforts across the organization. 

 

Enabling of single-sign-on and identity provisioning throughout your application landscape 

 

  1. Standardize Enterprise Automation 

Automating workflows and processes is essential for scaling AI-driven enterprise operations. Standardized automation supports consistent, efficient, and error-free processes. De Agostini Publishing S.p.A leveraged SAP BTP to automate vendor invoice management, enhancing accuracy, efficiency, and data reliability across new markets. Another example is from Colsubsidio’s SAP BTP implementation, which enables them efficient, independent analytics for strategic, data-driven decision-making. 

  • Automate Visually: Utilize intuitive, drag-and-drop interfaces and AI to simplify automation. 
  • Integrate Seamlessly: Speed up development with pre-built workflows, bots, and connectors for both SAP and non-SAP systems. 
  • Collaborate Securely: Empower cross-functional teams with secure, agile development tools. 

Automation Examples: 

  • Generative AI for Interactive Modeling: Supports modeling through natural language inputs with proactive validation. 
  • Document Information Extraction: Automates data capture from structured documents like invoices. 
  • Action Process AI (SAP Signavio): Combines large process models with SAP expertise to improve accuracy and effectiveness. 

Key Benefits: 

  • Reduced manual effort and error rates. 
  • Increased speed and quality of automation. 
  • Empowered cross-functional teams with collaboration capabilities. 

 

Process AI at SAP Signavio 

 

  1. Standardize Digital Experience Architecture for Conversational Experience 

To create a seamless digital experience, the architecture must support modularity and scalability, allowing for a unified user interface across devices and applications. 

  • Conversational UX: Enable conversational interfaces across desktop and mobile, enhancing the user experience with intuitive, end-to-end interactions. 
  • SAP Start for Cloud Solutions: A modular homepage offers a single point of entry with pre-configured access to cloud solutions like SAP SuccessFactors and S/4HANA Cloud. 
  • Joule in SAP Applications: SAP’s digital assistant, Joule, is embedded in applications to enhance user experience, starting with SAP Start and SAP SuccessFactors. 

AI-powered Talent Intelligence: 

  • Talent Optimization: Use AI to generate job descriptions and interview questions tailored to organizational needs, streamlining HR processes. 
  • Skills-Based Matching: Empower skills-based organizations with talent intelligence to identify and promote internal talent efficiently. 

Standardize UX Architecture – The SAP UX Story 

 

  1. Standardize Core - Building a Lean Core 

A lean core structure is essential to keep AI-enabled systems agile and cloud-compliant, enhancing scalability and reducing potential disruptions. 

  • Avoid Custom Extensions: Minimize customizations to ensure compatibility with SAP’s upgrade cycles. 
  • Separate Extensions with APIs: Ensure extensions do not interfere with upgrades by leveraging APIs. 
  • Cloud-Compliant Extensions: Develop with cloud compatibility in mind, aligning with a three-tier architectural model. 

Business Impact: 

  • Smooth upgrades, minimal disruptions. 
  • Scalable, cloud-ready architecture. 
  • Enhanced reliability in deployments. 

 

  1. Standardize Data Architecture 

Data architecture standardization is vital for ensuring seamless data sharing, comprehensive analytics, and powerful AI model training across the enterprise. 

  • Business Data Fabric: An architecture that delivers contextually rich data to every user, preserving business context and logic. 
  • Intelligent Data Applications: Enable developers to create apps that are AI-driven, context-aware, and seamlessly connected to core business data. 
  • Extended Planning and Analysis: Unify planning functions for finance, HR, and operations within a single, data-rich ecosystem. 
  • Data Sharing & Products: Facilitate data-sharing with data products that provide both abstraction and usability layers without introducing new artifacts. 

Example Use Case: 

  • Business Decision Simulator: Simulate complex scenarios to predict potential disruptions and make data-informed adjustments. 

Core Benefits: 

  • Holistic data access across departments. 
  • Stronger alignment between operational and strategic planning. 
  • Enhanced data-driven decision-making. 

 

  1. Standardize AI Architecture 

The AI architecture must support diverse AI capabilities, from natural language processing to generative AI, ensuring seamless integration and adaptability across SAP applications. 

  • Joule in All Apps: SAP’s digital assistant Joule, embedded in SAP applications, offers users insights and support while automating routine tasks. It allows users to directly access services like SAP SuccessFactors and S/4HANA Cloud. 
  • Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core: The GenAI Hub provides a central location for generative AI models, enabling SAP applications to draw upon AI capabilities like text generation, summarization, and process modeling in real time. 
  • SAP-Hosted Foundation Models: By hosting foundational AI models, SAP AI Core offers scalability and reliability, reducing dependency on external AI solutions and ensuring secure data handling. 

Benefits: 

  • Scalability: Supports consistent deployment across all SAP applications, reducing the need for fragmented, custom AI solutions. 
  • Data Security: Centralizes model hosting within SAP’s secure environment. 
  • Enhanced Process Intelligence: Infuses advanced process and data understanding across SAP systems, allowing companies to optimize AI-driven workflows effectively. 

Conclusion 

Standardizing architecture and tools across these six key areas creates a solid foundation for scalable, secure, and efficient AI adoption in enterprise settings. This standardization empowers organizations to move faster, adapt more readily to changes, and achieve a simplified and agile landscape that supports continued innovation and growth. 

 

Curious on a structured customer engagement model to set up standardized Cloud Architecture for Business AI Adoption? Connect with our Global BTP Customer Engagement Team at BTPFRE@sap.com.

entry.title: Re: ME21N CHECK ON WARNING RED-LIGHTS NEEDS TO STOP THE P...
entry.content: I could have guested that "the client has requested". The question is why they have requested that? Maybe they don't understand the concept of PO holding and no one has explained it to them.

entry.title: Re: SAP Personas , script OnEnter with unwanted "enter" f...
entry.content: Hello Cristin, It's posted. I replicate the problem with a simple flavor.

entry.title: Error in Adaptation Project with reuse component
entry.content:
Hi!

I have created a adaptation project for F2071 standard Fiori application and replaced the Odata service with enhanced one. List page is working fine but Object page is not loading due attachment service component usage at object page level. I have also tried using "Add UI5 reuse component" step but didn't worked. 

Any suggestions, how can I resolve this issue.

Thanks!

SAP Business Application Studio

entry.title: Re: How can the documents listed in the report 'RFDOPR20'...
entry.content: Dear Raymond Giuseppi, First of all, thank you very much for your support. As described in the link above, I have now populated the fields "BUKRS," "GJAHR," "AUGDT," "AUGBL," "BSCHL," "KOART," and "KUNNR" in the BSEG table accordingly. Unfortunately, I am still unable to match the amount displayed in the RFDOPR20 report. My assumption is that I may not have selected the correct booking keys from the TBSL table. One last question: Should all booking keys (TBSL-BSCHL) from the table be selected where the account type (TBSL-KOART = D) and revenue-relevant (TBSL-XUMSW = X) conditions are met? Thank you in advance for your feedback. Best regards, Emal
